name: Lawrence Welk
abstract: Lawrence Welk (March 11, 1903 – May 17, 1992) was an American accordionist, bandleader, and television impresario, who hosted The Lawrence Welk Show from 1951 to 1982. The program was known for its light and family-friendly style, and the easy listening music featured became known as "champagne music" to his radio, television, and live-p…

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Gilmore
about_quotes.evidence: we used to watch the “Lawrence Welk Show” together, because I liked the big band and they liked Lawrence Welk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Gilmore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Delaware Water Gap, PA, November 8, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Lawrence Welk was on the television... I knew pretty early on that Lawrence wasn’t for me.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: My mother used to watch t.v. She’d watch Lawrence Welk. She loved Lawrence Welk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: He did Lawrence Welk and — but the “Heartbreak Hotel” was, you know the echo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded Lawrence Welk at Madison Square Garden, that was a pretty popular album too. That was for Bob Theil. I was partied to all of that, I took the microphones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Howard R. Scott
about_quotes.evidence: Now, last night they had Lawrence Welk. Did you hear him on Channel Five? ... I was listening to them all by myself.
